---
title: "Question3"
author: "Bethelhem Kassa"
date: "2024-03-25"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)


#import Table cp-1 sheet from the excel file
statedf <- read_excel("C:/Users/nebar/Desktop/Dataderby/National+education+cost+data.xlsx",sheet = "TABLE CP-3", skip = 2)


#extract the data frame for public two years in district fees in 2023 dollars and rename the column names
statedf1 <- statedf[1:51,c(1:23)]|>
  rename("States" = `In 2023 Dollars...1`,
         "2004-05" = `2004-05...2`,
         "2005-06" = `2005-06...3`,
         "2006-07" = `2006-07...4`,
         "2007-08" = `2007-08...5`,
         "2008-09" = `2008-09...6`,
         "2009-10" = `2009-10...7`,
         "2010-11" = `2010-11...8`,
         "2011-12" = `2011-12...9`,
         "2012-13" = `2012-13...10`,
         "2013-14" = `2013-14...11`,
         "2014-15" = `2014-15...12`,
         "2015-16" = `2015-16...13`,
         "2016-17" = `2016-17...14`,
         "2017-18" = `2017-18...15`,
         "2018-19" = `2018-19...16`,
         "2019-20" = `2019-20...17`,
         "2020-21" = `2020-21...18`,
         "2021-22" = `2021-22...19`,
         "2022-23" = `2022-23...20`,
         "2023-24" = `2023-24...21`,
         "1-Year % Change" = `1-Year % Change...22`,
         "5-Year % Change" = `5-Year % Change...23`
         )
statedf1

#extract the data frame for public two years in district fees in current dollars and rename the column names
statedf2 <- statedf[54:104,c(1:23)]|>
  rename("States" = `In 2023 Dollars...1`,
         "2004-05" = `2004-05...2`,
         "2005-06" = `2005-06...3`,
         "2006-07" = `2006-07...4`,
         "2007-08" = `2007-08...5`,
         "2008-09" = `2008-09...6`,
         "2009-10" = `2009-10...7`,
         "2010-11" = `2010-11...8`,
         "2011-12" = `2011-12...9`,
         "2012-13" = `2012-13...10`,
         "2013-14" = `2013-14...11`,
         "2014-15" = `2014-15...12`,
         "2015-16" = `2015-16...13`,
         "2016-17" = `2016-17...14`,
         "2017-18" = `2017-18...15`,
         "2018-19" = `2018-19...16`,
         "2019-20" = `2019-20...17`,
         "2020-21" = `2020-21...18`,
         "2021-22" = `2021-22...19`,
         "2022-23" = `2022-23...20`,
         "2023-24" = `2023-24...21`,
         "1-Year % Change" = `1-Year % Change...22`,
         "5-Year % Change" = `5-Year % Change...23`
         )
statedf2
#extract the data frame for public four year in-state fees in 2023 dollars and rename the column names
statedf3 <- statedf[1:51,c(24:46)]|>
  rename("States" = `In 2023 Dollars...24`,
         "2004-05" = `2004-05...25`,
         "2005-06" = `2005-06...26`,
         "2006-07" = `2006-07...27`,
         "2007-08" = `2007-08...28`,
         "2008-09" = `2008-09...29`,
         "2009-10" = `2009-10...30`,
         "2010-11" = `2010-11...31`,
         "2011-12" = `2011-12...32`,
         "2012-13" = `2012-13...33`,
         "2013-14" = `2013-14...34`,
         "2014-15" = `2014-15...35`,
         "2015-16" = `2015-16...36`,
         "2016-17" = `2016-17...37`,
         "2017-18" = `2017-18...38`,
         "2018-19" = `2018-19...39`,
         "2019-20" = `2019-20...40`,
         "2020-21" = `2020-21...41`,
         "2021-22" = `2021-22...42`,
         "2022-23" = `2022-23...43`,
         "2023-24" = `2023-24...44`,
         "1-Year % Change" = `1-Year % Change...45`,
         "5-Year % Change" = `5-Year % Change...46`
         )
statedf3

#extract the data frame for public four year in-state fees in current dollars and rename the column names
statedf4 <- statedf[1:51,c(24:46)]|>
  rename("States" = `In 2023 Dollars...24`,
         "2004-05" = `2004-05...25`,
         "2005-06" = `2005-06...26`,
         "2006-07" = `2006-07...27`,
         "2007-08" = `2007-08...28`,
         "2008-09" = `2008-09...29`,
         "2009-10" = `2009-10...30`,
         "2010-11" = `2010-11...31`,
         "2011-12" = `2011-12...32`,
         "2012-13" = `2012-13...33`,
         "2013-14" = `2013-14...34`,
         "2014-15" = `2014-15...35`,
         "2015-16" = `2015-16...36`,
         "2016-17" = `2016-17...37`,
         "2017-18" = `2017-18...38`,
         "2018-19" = `2018-19...39`,
         "2019-20" = `2019-20...40`,
         "2020-21" = `2020-21...41`,
         "2021-22" = `2021-22...42`,
         "2022-23" = `2022-23...43`,
         "2023-24" = `2023-24...44`,
         "1-Year % Change" = `1-Year % Change...45`,
         "5-Year % Change" = `5-Year % Change...46`
         )
statedf4

#change all the columns except the first one to numeric
statedf1u <- cbind(statedf1$States,as.data.frame(apply(statedf1[2:23],2, as.numeric)))|>
  rename("States" = `statedf1$States`)

statedf2u <- cbind(statedf2$States,as.data.frame(apply(statedf2[2:23],2,as.numeric)))|>
  rename("States" = `statedf2$States`)

statedf3u <- cbind(statedf3$States,as.data.frame(apply(statedf3[2:23],2,as.numeric)))|>
  rename("States" = `statedf3$States`)

statedf4u <- cbind(statedf4$States,as.data.frame(apply(statedf4[2:23],2,as.numeric)))|>
  rename("States" = `statedf4$States`)


#find statistical summary for all the tables
summary(statedf1u)
summary(statedf2u)
summary(statedf3u)
summary(statedf4u)

#since Alaska and district Colombia don't have records for district fees needs to be removed
statedf1u1 <- statedf1u[-c(2,9),]
statedf2u1 <- statedf2u[-c(2,9),]
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)

df1 <- gather(statedf1u1,key = "Year", value = "fees", 2:21)
df2 <- gather(statedf2u1,key = "Year", value = "fees", 2:21)
df3 <- gather(statedf3u,key = "Year", value = "fees", 2:21)
df4 <- gather(statedf4u,key = "Year", value = "fees", 2:21)

#the most expensive states for public two-year in district fees in 2023 dollars
df1_exp <- df1|>
  filter(States  %in% c("Vermont","New Hampshire","South Dakota","Massachusetts","Minnesota"))
df1_exp
ggplotly(ggplot(df1, aes(x = Year, y=fees,group = States,color = States)) + geom_line() + labs(title = "Public two-year in district fees in 2023 dollars") + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)))
ggplotly(ggplot(df1_exp, aes(x = Year, y=fees,group = States,color = States)) + geom_line() + labs(title = "Top Five states with Expensive Public two-year colleges in-district fees ") + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)))

#the cheapest states
df1_chp <- df1|>
  filter(States  %in% c("California","New Mexico","North Carolina","Arizona","Texas"))
df1_chp
ggplotly(ggplot(df1_chp, aes(x = Year, y=fees,group = States,color = States)) + geom_line() + labs(title = "Top Five  states with Cheap Public two-year colleges in-district fees ") + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)))

ggplotly(ggplot(df2, aes(x = Year, y=fees,group = States,color = States)) + geom_line() + labs(title = "Public two-year in district fees in current dollars") + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)))
ggplotly(ggplot(df3, aes(x = Year, y=fees,group = States,color = States)) + geom_line()+ labs(title = "Public four-year in state fees in 2023 dollars") + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)))

#Top Five states with Expensive Public Four-year colleges in-district fees
df3_exp <- df3|>
  filter(States  %in% c("Vermont","New Hampshire","Pennsylvania","New Jersey","Illinois"))
df3_exp
ggplotly(ggplot(df3_exp, aes(x = Year, y=fees,group = States,color = States)) + geom_line() + labs(title = "Top Five states with Expensive Public Four-year colleges in-district fees ") + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)))

df3_chp <- df3|>
  filter(States  %in% c("Wyoming","Florida","Utah","North Carolina","New Mexico"))
df3_chp
ggplotly(ggplot(df3_chp, aes(x = Year, y=fees,group = States,color = States)) + geom_line() + labs(title = "Top Five  states with Cheap Public Four-year colleges in-district fees ") + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)))

ggplotly(ggplot(df4, aes(x = Year, y=fees,group = States,color = States)) + geom_line()+ labs(title = "Public four-year in state fees in current dollars") + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)))

ggplotly(ggplot(df1, aes(x = factor(States), y = fees))+ 
  geom_bar(stat = "Summary", fun = "mean",fill = "steelblue") + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)) + labs(title = "Public two years in district average fees in 2023 dollars"))


ggplotly(ggplot(df3, aes(x = factor(States), y = fees))+ 
  geom_bar(stat = "Summary", fun = "mean",fill = "steelblue") + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)) + labs(title = "Public four years in district average fees in 2023 dollars"))
```
```{r}
df1u <- df1$fees
df1u
df2u <- df2$fees

#to help the comparison b|n the two tables, takeout Alaska and District of Columbia records
df3u <- df3|>
  group_by(States)|>
  filter(States != "Alaska" & States != "District of Columbia" )
df3u_m <- df3u$fees

df4u <- df4|>
  group_by(States)|>
  filter(States != "Alaska" & States != "District of Columbia" )
df4u
df4u_m <- df4u$fees

#compare the average between the tables
college_df <- data.frame(group = rep(c("Two_year","Four_year"),each = 980), fees = c(df1u,df3u_m))
college_df
college_sum <- group_by(college_df,group)|>
  summarise(
    mean = mean(fees,na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(fees,na.rm = TRUE)
  )
college_sum
ggplot(college_df,aes(x = group,y= fees)) + geom_boxplot() + labs(title = "comparison between public two-year and four-year ")

#H0: mu(two) = mu(four);mu(two) != mu(four)

college_t <- t.test(fees ~ group,data = college_df, alternative = "two.sided",paired = TRUE)
college_t
# since t = 78.982 and p-value < 0.05, reject H0
#Therefore, there is evidence to support a significant difference between the average of two-college and four-college
```
